title: Impossible Possible: Overcoming Medical Crisis
mainTheme: Medical Crisis Overcome - Proving the Impossible Possible
struggleMoment: Sudden medical crisis strikes - meningitis at 19 with 2% survival chance, or cancer at 13 with 3 months to live. Body betrays, life changes in hours. Near-death experience, loss of body parts/function, being told you're going to die.
perseveranceMoments: Amy: Purple feet, hands, nose - 'I just knew I was dying' moment
Sean: Hair falling out in shower, collapsing to floor, tears running down face
Amy: Being wheeled into amputation surgery, terrified
Sean: Bone marrow extraction - most painful experience
Amy: First time on prosthetics - reality of lifelong journey hits
Sean: Second cancer diagnosis at 16 - '14 days to live'
Amy: Chairlift fear - 'What if I can't do this?'
Sean: Going to bed not knowing if he'd wake up
Amy: Legs flying off down the mountain - 'This is why you don't see people with prosthetic legs snowboarding'
Sean: Living in car seeking Everest sponsorship, everyone saying impossible
Amy: Dancing with Stars - world expecting her to fail, Twitter criticism
Sean: Camp 3 cerebral edema - vomiting, pushing through
Amy: Calling manufacturers, trying different prosthetic feet, nothing working
Sean: 'Eight months doesn't make a Himalayan climber' - proving doubters wrong
breakthroughMoment: Realizing 'maybe I'm the person passionate enough to figure this out' - choosing to focus on living not dying, proving impossible possible through determination and creativity. Achieving what no one thought possible - Paralympic snowboarding, Dancing with Stars, climbing Everest with one lung.
keyEmotionalMoments: Feeling sick, thinking it's the flu - normal day turns into crisis
Purple feet, hands, nose - 'I just knew I was dying' (Amy)
Lungs collapsing on way to hospital - fighting to breathe
Parents told 'she has two hours left to live' (Amy) / 'your firstborn son has three months to live' (Sean)
Waking from coma, seeing black/purple feet
Being wheeled into amputation surgery - terrified, no idea what life will be like
Hair falling out in shower - collapsing to floor, tears, pulling chunks from drain (Sean)
Thinking about what friends were doing that morning - 'I could either fight or die'
Second cancer diagnosis - '14 days to live', going to bed not knowing if you'll wake up
Seeing photo of amputee runner - 'If she can run, I can snowboard' (Amy)
Chairlift fear - 'What if I can't do this? This moment will tell me'
Legs flying off down the mountain - 'This is why you don't see people with prosthetic legs snowboarding'
Defeated moment - 'Clearly, it's not possible' then shift: 'Maybe I'm the person passionate enough to figure it out'
Living in car seeking sponsorship - everyone saying impossible (Sean)
Dancing with Stars - world expecting failure, Twitter criticism
Calling manufacturer: 'I need feet to dance' - 'They're made for swimming' - creative solution
Climbing Everest with one lung - 'If some schmuck with one lung can climb Everest, you can do anything'
Sydney child who died but was inspired - purpose transformation
characterName: Sarah (female, meningitis/amputation story), Jake (male, cancer/Everest story)
notes: Composite story combining elements from Amy Purdy (meningitis, lost legs, snowboarding, Dancing with Stars) and Sean Swarner (cancer twice, lost lung, Everest climb). Two parallel narratives showing different medical crises overcome through determination. Focus on 'proving impossible possible' theme.
